sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) taking possession, occupation of any thing, Isa 17:11, בְּיֹום נַחֲלָה “in the day of occupation,” of occupying the harvest, [“but on account of the following words, the reading נַחְלָה wound, is to be preferred; see חָלָה Niphal”]; also, possession, domain, Num 18:21. Often used of the territory in the Holy Land assigned to the respective tribes, e.g. Jos 13:23, נַחֲלַת בְּנֵי רְאוּבֵן “the possession of the Reubenites;” Num 18:23 26:62 27:7 also used of the whole of the Holy Land which was given to the Israelites, Deu 4:21. נַחֲלַת יְהֹוָה is-(a) the especial possession of Jehovah, i.e. Israel, for whom Jehovah cared and watched as being his own, Deu 4:20 9:26 , 29 Psal. 28:9 .-(b) a possession granted by Jehovah, the gift of Jehovah, Psa 127:3. As to the phrase יֵשׁ לי חֵלֶק וְנַחֲלָה בְּ, see חֵלֶק No. 2, .

(2) inheritance, 1Ki 21:3, 1Ki 21:4. Pro 19:14, נַחֲלַת אָבֹות “an inheritance received from fathers.”

(3) a lot assigned by God, i.q. חֵלֶק No. 2, Job 20:29 27:13 31:2 .
